en.extract:
The upland pipit (Anthus sylvanus) is a species of bird in the family Motacillidae.
It is found in Afghanistan, China, Hong Kong, India, Nepal, and Pakistan. As its name suggests, it inhabits mountains, typically open areas with grassland at elevations above 500m.

The '''upland pipit''' ('''''Anthus sylvanus''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Motacillidae]].
It is found in [[Afghanistan]], [[China]], [[Hong Kong]], [[India]], [[Nepal]], and [[Pakistan]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Upland Pipit (Anthus sylvanus ) |url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/species/uplpip1/cur/introduction |website=birdsoftheworld.org |publisher=CornellLab |access-date=19 July 2025}}</ref> As its name suggests, it inhabits mountains, typically open areas with grassland at elevations above 500m.

zh.extract:
山鹨（学名：Anthus sylvanus）为鶺鸰科鹨属的鸟类。分布于东阿富汗、尼泊尔、印度以及中国大陆的四川、云南、广西、江西、浙江、福建、广东等地，多见于山林、草地、农田以及喜在峻峭的山坡、岩石间或灌木丛中。该物种的模式产地在尼泊尔。
